message:
feat: Implement upgrade command verification after release (bd-1jt.5.6)

Add dsr verify upgrade command for post-release verification:
- src/upgrade_verify.sh module with upgrade check verification
- Tests tool upgrade --check command works correctly
- Detects asset naming mismatches between build and upgrade code
- Supports building from source, dry-run, JSON output

dsr verify upgrade subcommands:
- <tool> - Verify single tool's upgrade command
- --all - Verify all tools from repos.d
- --build-from-source - Build before testing
- --dry-run - Show what would be tested

Tests: 14 tests covering module loading, tool verification,
upgrade check parsing, JSON output, and repository finding.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1jt.5.17","title":"Write tests for supply chain security (SLSA, SBOM, quality gates)","description":"# Tests for Supply Chain Security Features

## Purpose
Test SLSA provenance attestation, SBOM generation, and pre-release quality gates.

## Test Coverage

### SLSA Provenance Tests (tests/unit/test_slsa.bats)
```bash
@test "slsa_generate creates valid in-toto statement" {
  local artifact="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/test-binary"
  echo "binary" > "$artifact"
  
  run slsa_generate "$artifact" --builder "dsr/v1"
  assert_success
  
  # Verify JSON structure
  local stmt
  stmt="$(cat "${artifact}.intoto.jsonl")"
  assert_equal "$(echo "$stmt" | jq -r '._type')" "https://in-toto.io/Statement/v1"
  assert_equal "$(echo "$stmt" | jq -r '.predicateType')" "https://slsa.dev/provenance/v1"
}

@test "slsa_generate includes correct subject digest" {
  local artifact="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/test-binary"
  echo "binary content" > "$artifact"
  local expected_sha256
  expected_sha256="$(sha256sum "$artifact" | cut -d' ' -f1)"
  
  run slsa_generate "$artifact"
  assert_success
  
  local actual_sha256
  actual_sha256="$(cat "${artifact}.intoto.jsonl" | jq -r '.subject[0].digest.sha256')"
  assert_equal "$actual_sha256" "$expected_sha256"
}

@test "slsa_verify validates attestation" {
  # Create artifact + valid attestation
  local artifact="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/test-binary"
  echo "binary" > "$artifact"
  slsa_generate "$artifact"
  
  run slsa_verify "$artifact"
  assert_success
}

@test "slsa_verify fails on tampered artifact" {
  local artifact="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/test-binary"
  echo "binary" > "$artifact"
  slsa_generate "$artifact"
  
  # Tamper with artifact
  echo "modified" > "$artifact"
  
  run slsa_verify "$artifact"
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "digest mismatch"
}
```

### SBOM Tests (tests/unit/test_sbom.bats)
```bash
@test "sbom_generate creates SPDX JSON" {
  local project="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/project"
  mkdir -p "$project"
  echo '[package]
name = "test-crate"
version = "1.0.0"' > "$project/Cargo.toml"
  
  run sbom_generate "$project" --format spdx
  assert_success
  assert [ -f "$project/sbom.spdx.json" ]
  
  # Verify SPDX structure
  assert_equal "$(jq -r '.spdxVersion' "$project/sbom.spdx.json")" "SPDX-2.3"
}

@test "sbom_generate creates CycloneDX JSON" {
  local project="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/project"
  mkdir -p "$project"
  echo '{"name": "test-pkg", "version": "1.0.0"}' > "$project/package.json"
  
  run sbom_generate "$project" --format cyclonedx
  assert_success
  assert [ -f "$project/sbom.cdx.json" ]
}

@test "sbom_generate handles missing deps gracefully" {
  local project="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/empty"
  mkdir -p "$project"
  
  run sbom_generate "$project"
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "No package manifest found"
}
```

### Quality Gates Tests (tests/unit/test_quality_gates.bats)
```bash
@test "quality_gates runs configured checks" {
  # Mock check commands
  function cargo() { echo "Check passed"; return 0; }
  function go() { echo "Go vet passed"; return 0; }
  export -f cargo go
  
  run quality_gates "ntm" --config "$FIXTURES/repos-with-checks.yaml"
  assert_success
  assert_output --partial "All checks passed"
}

@test "quality_gates fails on blocking check failure" {
  function cargo() { echo "Error: unused import"; return 1; }
  export -f cargo
  
  run quality_gates "test-tool" --config "$FIXTURES/repos-with-checks.yaml"
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "Blocking check failed"
}

@test "quality_gates continues on allow_fail checks" {
  # lint fails but is in allow_fail
  function lint_tool() { return 1; }
  function test_tool() { return 0; }
  export -f lint_tool test_tool
  
  run quality_gates "test-tool" --config "$FIXTURES/repos-allow-fail.yaml"
  assert_success
  assert_output --partial "Non-blocking check failed: lint"
}

@test "quality_gates skipped with --skip-checks" {
  run quality_gates "ntm" --skip-checks
  assert_success
  assert_output --partial "Quality gates skipped"
}

@test "quality_gates produces JSON report" {
  run quality_gates "ntm" --json
  assert_success
  
  # Verify JSON structure
  echo "$output" | jq -e '.checks | length > 0'
  echo "$output" | jq -e '.passed == true'
}
```

### Integration Tests

```bash
  # Setup mock release
  run dsr release test-tool v1.0.0 --dry-run --with-slsa --with-sbom
  assert_success
  assert_output --partial "Would upload: test-tool.intoto.jsonl"
  assert_output --partial "Would upload: sbom.spdx.json"
}
```

## Purpose
Test that dsr commands work correctly with real (mocked) GH API responses and tool interactions, while preserving stream separation and JSON validity.

## Test File
`tests/integration/test_commands.bats`

## Test Cases (minimum)

### dsr check
```bash
@test "dsr check detects queued run over threshold" {
  export GH_MOCK_RESPONSE='[{"status":"queued","createdAt":"2026-01-30T12:00:00Z"}]'
  run dsr check ntm
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "THROTTLED"
}
```

### dsr watch
```bash
@test "dsr watch --once returns valid JSON" {
  run dsr watch --once --json
  echo "$output" | jq .
}
```

### dsr repos
```bash
@test "dsr repos list shows all 16 tools" {
  run dsr repos list
  assert_success
  assert_output --partial "ntm"
}
```

### dsr build (dry-run)
```bash
@test "dsr build --dry-run shows planned actions" {
  run dsr build ntm --dry-run
  assert_success
```

```bash
@test "dsr release --dry-run shows upload plan" {
  mkdir -p /tmp/test-artifacts
  touch /tmp/test-artifacts/ntm-linux-amd64
  run dsr release ntm v1.0.0 --artifacts /tmp/test-artifacts --dry-run
  assert_success

}
```

 {"id":"bd-1jt.5.4","title":"Implement comprehensive logging infrastructure for dsr","description":"# Comprehensive Logging Infrastructure for dsr

## Purpose
Provide deterministic, structured logs for every action so failures are diagnosable, retries are safe, and audit trails exist for releases.

## Core Principles
- **Structured JSON Lines** for machine parsing
- **stderr** for human logs (colored), **stdout** reserved for JSON output
- **No \`set -e\`**: explicit error handling with captured exit codes
- Stable \`DSR_RUN_ID\` to correlate events across commands
- **Verbosity levels**: error, warn, info, debug (configurable via -v/-q flags)

## File: src/logging.sh
\`\`\`bash
#\!/usr/bin/env bash
# logging.sh - Structured logging for dsr

set -uo pipefail

# Log levels (numeric for comparison)
declare -A LOG_LEVELS=(
  [error]=0
  [warn]=1
  [info]=2
  [debug]=3
)

# Current log level (default: info)
LOG_LEVEL="${DSR_LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
LOG_FILE="${DSR_LOG_FILE:-}"

# Colors (disabled if not TTY or NO_COLOR set)
if [[ -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" && -t 2 ]]; then
  _RED=$'\033[0;31m' _YELLOW=$'\033[0;33m'
  _GREEN=$'\033[0;32m' _BLUE=$'\033[0;34m'
  _GRAY=$'\033[0;90m' _NC=$'\033[0m'
else
  _RED='' _YELLOW='' _GREEN='' _BLUE='' _GRAY='' _NC=''
fi

# Generate run ID if not set
: "${DSR_RUN_ID:="run-$(date +%s)-$$"}"

# Initialize logging
log_init() {
  local state_dir="${DSR_STATE_DIR:-$HOME/.local/state/dsr}"
  local log_dir="$state_dir/logs"
  mkdir -p "$log_dir"
  
  # Set log file if not specified
  if [[ -z "$LOG_FILE" ]]; then
    LOG_FILE="$log_dir/dsr-$(date +%Y%m%d).log"
  fi
  
  # Rotate logs older than 30 days
  find "$log_dir" -name 'dsr-*.log' -mtime +30 -delete 2>/dev/null || true
  
  # Compress logs older than 7 days
  find "$log_dir" -name 'dsr-*.log' -mtime +7 \! -name '*.gz' \
    -exec gzip -q {} \; 2>/dev/null || true
}

# Check if level should be logged
_should_log() {
  local level="$1"
  [[ "${LOG_LEVELS[$level]}" -le "${LOG_LEVELS[$LOG_LEVEL]}" ]]
}

# Core log function
_log() {
  local level="$1" msg="$2"
  shift 2
  local extras="$*"
  
  _should_log "$level" || return 0
  
  local ts
  ts="$(date -Iseconds)"
  
  # Build JSON log entry
  local json="{"
  json+="\"ts\":\"$ts\","
  json+="\"run_id\":\"$DSR_RUN_ID\","
  json+="\"level\":\"$level\","
  json+="\"cmd\":\"${DSR_CURRENT_CMD:-}\","
  json+="\"msg\":\"$msg\""
  
  # Add optional context fields
  [[ -n "${DSR_CURRENT_TOOL:-}" ]] && json+=",\"tool\":\"$DSR_CURRENT_TOOL\""
  [[ -n "${DSR_CURRENT_HOST:-}" ]] && json+=",\"host\":\"$DSR_CURRENT_HOST\""
  [[ -n "$extras" ]] && json+=",$extras"
  json+="}"
  
  # Write to log file
  [[ -n "$LOG_FILE" ]] && echo "$json" >> "$LOG_FILE"
  
  # Human-readable stderr output
  local color
  case "$level" in
    error) color="$_RED" ;;
    warn)  color="$_YELLOW" ;;
    info)  color="$_GREEN" ;;
    debug) color="$_GRAY" ;;
  esac
  
  echo "${color}[$level]${_NC} $msg" >&2
}

# Convenience functions
log_error() { _log error "$1" "${2:-}"; }
log_warn()  { _log warn "$1" "${2:-}"; }
log_info()  { _log info "$1" "${2:-}"; }
log_debug() { _log debug "$1" "${2:-}"; }

# Log with duration tracking
log_timed() {
  local start_ts=$(date +%s%3N)
  "$@"
  local exit_code=$?
  local end_ts=$(date +%s%3N)
  local duration_ms=$((end_ts - start_ts))
  
  log_info "Completed: $1" "\"duration_ms\":$duration_ms,\"exit_code\":$exit_code"
  return $exit_code
}

# Export functions
export -f log_init log_error log_warn log_info log_debug log_timed
export DSR_RUN_ID LOG_LEVEL LOG_FILE
\`\`\`

## Log Locations
- \`~/.local/state/dsr/logs/dsr-YYYYMMDD.log\` - Daily session logs
- \`~/.local/state/dsr/builds/<tool>/<version>/<run_id>/\` - Per-build logs
- \`~/.local/state/dsr/builds/<tool>/<version>/<run_id>/<host>.log\` - Per-host build logs

## Log Schema (JSONL)
\`\`\`json
{"ts":"2026-01-30T13:14:22Z","run_id":"run-1738246462-12345","level":"info","cmd":"build","tool":"ntm","msg":"Starting build","host":"trj"}
{"ts":"2026-01-30T13:14:25Z","run_id":"run-1738246462-12345","level":"info","cmd":"build","tool":"ntm","msg":"Completed: go build","duration_ms":3200,"exit_code":0}
{"ts":"2026-01-30T13:14:26Z","run_id":"run-1738246462-12345","level":"error","cmd":"build","tool":"ntm","msg":"Build failed","exit_code":1}
\`\`\`

## Required Fields
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| ts | ISO8601 | Yes | Timestamp |
| run_id | string | Yes | Correlation ID |
| level | enum | Yes | error/warn/info/debug |
| cmd | string | Yes | Current command |
| msg | string | Yes | Log message |
| tool | string | Contextual | Tool being processed |
| host | string | Contextual | Build host |
| duration_ms | int | Timed ops | Operation duration |
| exit_code | int | On completion | Exit code |

## Retention Policy
- **Keep**: Last 30 days of session logs
- **Compress**: Logs older than 7 days (gzip)
- **Delete**: Logs older than 30 days

- [ ] log_error/warn/info/debug respect LOG_LEVEL
- [ ] log_timed captures duration and exit code
- [ ] stderr gets colored human-readable output
- [ ] log file gets JSONL machine-readable output
- [ ] run_id correlates all logs from single invocation

-{"id":"bd-1jt.5.6","title":"Implement upgrade command verification after release","description":"# Upgrade Command Verification

## Purpose
After releasing binaries, verify that each tool's `upgrade --check` command correctly finds and downloads the new release assets. Catches asset naming mismatches between code and build configs.

## Why This Matters
- Go tools use upgrade.go to detect GOOS/GOARCH and fetch assets
- Rust tools use self-update crates
- Asset naming must match exactly between build and upgrade code
- This verifies the contract end-to-end

## Test Matrix
Run on each platform after release:
| Platform | Command | Expected |
|----------|---------|----------|
| Linux amd64 | `tool upgrade --check` | Found asset matching linux-amd64 |
| Linux arm64 | `tool upgrade --check` | Found asset matching linux-arm64 |
| macOS arm64 | `tool upgrade --check` | Found asset matching darwin-arm64 |
| macOS amd64 | `tool upgrade --check` | Found asset matching darwin-amd64 |
| Windows amd64 | `tool upgrade --check` | Found asset matching windows-amd64 |

## Implementation (No global cd)
```bash
verify_upgrade() {
  local tool="$1"
  local version="$2"
  local repo_dir="/data/projects/$tool"
  local bin_path="$repo_dir/.dsr-tmp/$tool"

  mkdir -p "$repo_dir/.dsr-tmp"

  # Build tool from source first
  if [ -f "$repo_dir/go.mod" ]; then
    go build -o "$bin_path" "$repo_dir/cmd/$tool" 2>/dev/null
  else
    cargo build --release --manifest-path "$repo_dir/Cargo.toml"
    cp "$repo_dir/target/release/$tool" "$bin_path"
  fi

  # Run upgrade check
  output=$("$bin_path" upgrade --check 2>&1)

  if echo "$output" | grep -q "no suitable release asset"; then
    echo "ERROR: Asset naming mismatch for $tool on $(uname -s)/$(uname -m)"
    return 1
  fi

  echo "✓ Upgrade check passed for $tool"
}
```

## dsr Command
```bash
dsr verify upgrade <tool>         # Verify on current platform
dsr verify upgrade <tool> --all   # Verify on all platforms via SSH
```

 {"id":"bd-1jt.5.9","title":"Test harness: deterministic time/random + log capture helpers","description":"# Test Harness: Deterministic Time/Random + Log Capture Helpers

## Purpose
Eliminate flaky tests by controlling time and randomness, and ensure logs are captured for debugging failed tests.

## File Structure
```
tests/
├── helpers/
│   ├── test_harness.bash        # Core harness sourced by all tests
│   ├── mock_time.bash           # Time freezing utilities
│   ├── mock_random.bash         # Seeded random utilities
│   ├── log_capture.bash         # Log capture and assertion helpers
│   ├── mock_ssh.bash            # SSH command mocking
│   ├── mock_gh.bash             # GitHub API mocking
│   └── mock_common.bash         # Shared mock infrastructure
├── fixtures/
│   ├── sample_config.yaml       # Test configuration fixtures
│   ├── mock_gh_responses/       # Canned API responses
│   └── sample_repos/            # Minimal repo structures for testing
└── bats.config.bash             # Global bats configuration
```

## Core Test Harness (tests/helpers/test_harness.bash)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# test_harness.bash - Core test harness for all dsr tests

# Load all helper modules
HELPERS_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
source "$HELPERS_DIR/mock_time.bash"
source "$HELPERS_DIR/mock_random.bash"
source "$HELPERS_DIR/log_capture.bash"
source "$HELPERS_DIR/mock_common.bash"

# Global test setup
harness_setup() {
  # Create isolated test environment
  export TEST_RUN_ID="test-$(date +%s)-$$"
  export TEST_TMPDIR="$(mktemp -d)"
  export DSR_CONFIG_DIR="$TEST_TMPDIR/config"
  export DSR_STATE_DIR="$TEST_TMPDIR/state"
  export DSR_CACHE_DIR="$TEST_TMPDIR/cache"
  mkdir -p "$DSR_CONFIG_DIR" "$DSR_STATE_DIR" "$DSR_CACHE_DIR"
  
  # Initialize log capture
  log_capture_init "$TEST_TMPDIR/test.log"
  
  # Freeze time to known value
  mock_time_freeze "2026-01-30T12:00:00Z"
  
  # Seed random
  mock_random_seed 42
  
  echo "=== Test: ${BATS_TEST_NAME:-unknown} ==="
  echo "=== Run ID: $TEST_RUN_ID ==="
  echo "=== Started: $(date -Iseconds) ==="
}

# Global test teardown
harness_teardown() {
  local exit_code=${BATS_ERROR_STATUS:-0}
  
  echo "=== Ended: $(date -Iseconds) ==="
  echo "=== Exit: $exit_code ==="
  
  # On failure, dump logs
  if [ "$exit_code" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "=== TEST FAILED - Full Log Output ==="
    log_capture_dump
    echo "=== End Log Output ==="
    echo ""
  fi
  
  # Restore time/random
  mock_time_restore
  mock_random_restore
  
  # Cleanup (unless DEBUG mode)
  if [ -z "${DEBUG:-}" ]; then
    rm -rf "$TEST_TMPDIR"
  else
    echo "Debug mode: test artifacts preserved at $TEST_TMPDIR"
  fi
}

export -f harness_setup harness_teardown
```

## Time Mocking (tests/helpers/mock_time.bash)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# mock_time.bash - Deterministic time for tests

_ORIGINAL_DATE=
_MOCK_TIME=

mock_time_freeze() {
  local timestamp="$1"
  _MOCK_TIME="$timestamp"
  _ORIGINAL_DATE="$(which date)"
  
  # Create mock date function
  date() {
    if [[ "$1" == "+%s" ]]; then
      # Return epoch seconds for frozen time
      "$_ORIGINAL_DATE" -d "$_MOCK_TIME" +%s 2>/dev/null || \
      "$_ORIGINAL_DATE" -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ" "$_MOCK_TIME" +%s
    elif [[ "$1" == "-Iseconds" ]]; then
      echo "$_MOCK_TIME"
    else
      # Pass through other formats
      "$_ORIGINAL_DATE" "$@"
    fi
  }
  export -f date
}

mock_time_advance() {
  local seconds="$1"
  # Advance frozen time by N seconds
  local current_epoch
  current_epoch=$("$_ORIGINAL_DATE" -d "$_MOCK_TIME" +%s 2>/dev/null)
  local new_epoch=$((current_epoch + seconds))
  _MOCK_TIME="$("$_ORIGINAL_DATE" -d "@$new_epoch" -Iseconds 2>/dev/null)"
}

mock_time_restore() {
  unset -f date 2>/dev/null || true
  _MOCK_TIME=
}
```

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# mock_random.bash - Seeded random for deterministic tests

_RANDOM_SEED=0
_RANDOM_COUNTER=0

mock_random_seed() {
  _RANDOM_SEED="$1"
  _RANDOM_COUNTER=0
}

# Deterministic random number (linear congruential generator)
mock_random() {
  local max="${1:-32768}"
  _RANDOM_COUNTER=$(( (_RANDOM_SEED * 1103515245 + 12345 + _RANDOM_COUNTER) % 2147483648 ))
  echo $(( _RANDOM_COUNTER % max ))
}

# Deterministic UUID
mock_uuid() {
  printf '%08x-%04x-%04x-%04x-%012x' \
    $(mock_random 4294967296) \
    $(mock_random 65536) \
    $(mock_random 65536) \
    $(mock_random 65536) \
    $(mock_random 281474976710656)
}

mock_random_restore() {
  _RANDOM_SEED=0
  _RANDOM_COUNTER=0
}
```

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# log_capture.bash - Capture and assert on logs

_LOG_FILE=
_LOG_FD_STDOUT=
_LOG_FD_STDERR=

log_capture_init() {
  _LOG_FILE="$1"
  
  # Save original stdout/stderr
  exec {_LOG_FD_STDOUT}>&1 {_LOG_FD_STDERR}>&2
  
  # Redirect to log file (tee to preserve output)
  exec > >(tee -a "$_LOG_FILE") 2>&1
}

log_capture_dump() {
  if [ -f "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
    cat "$_LOG_FILE"
  fi
}

log_capture_contains() {
  local pattern="$1"
  grep -q "$pattern" "$_LOG_FILE"
}

log_capture_line_count() {
  wc -l < "$_LOG_FILE" | tr -d ' '
}

log_capture_restore() {
  exec 1>&${_LOG_FD_STDOUT} 2>&${_LOG_FD_STDERR}
  exec {_LOG_FD_STDOUT}>&- {_LOG_FD_STDERR}>&-
}

export -f log_capture_init log_capture_dump log_capture_contains log_capture_line_count log_capture_restore
```

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# src/upgrade_verify.sh - Verify tool upgrade commands work correctly
+#
+# bd-1jt.5.6: Implement upgrade command verification after release
+#
+# After releasing binaries, verify that each tool's `upgrade --check`
+# command correctly finds and downloads the new release assets.
+#
+# Usage:
+#   source "$SCRIPT_DIR/src/upgrade_verify.sh"
+#   upgrade_verify_tool ntm
+#   upgrade_verify_all
+#
+# Required modules:
+#   - logging.sh (for log_info, log_error, etc.)
+#   - config.sh (for repos config)
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Configuration
+# ============================================================================
+
+# Default timeout for upgrade check (seconds)
+UPGRADE_VERIFY_TIMEOUT=30
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Verification Functions
+# ============================================================================
+
+# Verify upgrade command works for a tool
+# Args: tool_name [--version <ver>] [--build-from-source]
+# Returns: 0 on success, 1 on failure
+upgrade_verify_tool() {
+    local tool_name="${1:-}"
+    shift 2>/dev/null || true
+
+    local version=""
+    local build_from_source=false
+    local dry_run=false
+
+    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+        case "$1" in
+            --version|-V) version="$2"; shift 2 ;;
+            --build-from-source) build_from_source=true; shift ;;
+            --dry-run) dry_run=true; shift ;;
+            *) shift ;;
+        esac
+    done
+
+    if [[ -z "$tool_name" ]]; then
+        log_error "Tool name required"
+        return 4
+    fi
+
+    log_info "Verifying upgrade command for $tool_name..."
+
+    # Find the tool binary
+    local bin_path=""
+
+    # Check if installed in PATH
+    if command -v "$tool_name" &>/dev/null; then
+        bin_path=$(command -v "$tool_name")
+        log_debug "Found $tool_name at: $bin_path"
+    fi
+
+    # Build from source if requested or not found
+    if $build_from_source || [[ -z "$bin_path" ]]; then
+        local repo_dir
+        repo_dir=$(_upgrade_find_repo_dir "$tool_name")
+
+        if [[ -z "$repo_dir" || ! -d "$repo_dir" ]]; then
+            log_error "Repository not found for $tool_name"
+            return 1
+        fi
+
+        log_info "Building $tool_name from source..."
+        bin_path=$(_upgrade_build_tool "$tool_name" "$repo_dir")
+
+        if [[ -z "$bin_path" || ! -x "$bin_path" ]]; then
+            log_error "Failed to build $tool_name"
+            return 1
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -z "$bin_path" ]]; then
+        log_error "$tool_name not found in PATH and --build-from-source not specified"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    # Run upgrade check
+    log_info "Running upgrade check..."
+    local output exit_code=0
+
+    if $dry_run; then
+        log_info "[DRY RUN] Would run: $bin_path upgrade --check"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    output=$(timeout "$UPGRADE_VERIFY_TIMEOUT" "$bin_path" upgrade --check 2>&1) || exit_code=$?
+
+    # Parse output
+    local found_asset=false
+    local current_version=""
+    local latest_version=""
+
+    # Check for common success patterns
+    if echo "$output" | grep -qi "up.to.date\|no update\|already.*latest\|current version"; then
+        found_asset=true
+        log_ok "$tool_name upgrade --check: Up to date"
+    elif echo "$output" | grep -qi "found.*asset\|download.*available\|update.*available"; then
+        found_asset=true
+        log_ok "$tool_name upgrade --check: Found update"
+    elif echo "$output" | grep -qi "no suitable release asset\|asset not found\|failed to find"; then
+        found_asset=false
+        log_error "$tool_name upgrade --check: Asset naming mismatch"
+        log_error "Output: $output"
+        return 1
+    elif [[ $exit_code -eq 0 ]]; then
+        # Exit 0 usually means success
+        found_asset=true
+        log_ok "$tool_name upgrade --check: Completed successfully"
+    fi
+
+    # Extract versions if present
+    current_version=$(echo "$output" | grep -oP 'current.*?(\d+\.\d+\.\d+)' | grep -oP '\d+\.\d+\.\d+' | head -1)
+    latest_version=$(echo "$output" | grep -oP 'latest.*?(\d+\.\d+\.\d+)' | grep -oP '\d+\.\d+\.\d+' | head -1)
+
+    if [[ -n "$current_version" ]]; then
+        log_debug "Current version: $current_version"
+    fi
+    if [[ -n "$latest_version" ]]; then
+        log_debug "Latest version: $latest_version"
+    fi
+
+    # Build result
+    local platform
+    platform="$(uname -s | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')/$(uname -m)"
+    case "$platform" in
+        linux/x86_64) platform="linux/amd64" ;;
+        linux/aarch64) platform="linux/arm64" ;;
+        darwin/arm64) platform="darwin/arm64" ;;
+        darwin/x86_64) platform="darwin/amd64" ;;
+    esac
+
+    if $found_asset; then
+        log_ok "Upgrade verification PASSED for $tool_name on $platform"
+        return 0
+    else
+        log_error "Upgrade verification FAILED for $tool_name on $platform"
+        log_error "Exit code: $exit_code"
+        log_error "Output: $output"
+        return 1
+    fi
+}
+
+# Verify upgrade command for all tools
+# Args: [--build-from-source]
+upgrade_verify_all() {
+    local build_from_source=false
+    local dry_run=false
+
+    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+        case "$1" in
+            --build-from-source) build_from_source=true; shift ;;
+            --dry-run) dry_run=true; shift ;;
+            *) shift ;;
+        esac
+    done
+
+    local repos_dir="${ACT_REPOS_DIR:-${DSR_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.config/dsr}/repos.d}"
+    local results=()
+    local passed=0 failed=0 skipped=0
+
+    if [[ ! -d "$repos_dir" ]]; then
+        log_error "repos.d directory not found: $repos_dir"
+        return 4
+    fi
+
+    log_info "Verifying upgrade commands for all tools..."
+
+    local config_file tool_name
+    for config_file in "$repos_dir"/*.yaml; do
+        [[ -f "$config_file" ]] || continue
+        [[ "$(basename "$config_file")" == _* ]] && continue
+
+        tool_name=$(basename "$config_file" .yaml)
+
+        # Check if tool has upgrade capability
+        local has_upgrade=false
+        if command -v "$tool_name" &>/dev/null; then
+            if "$tool_name" --help 2>&1 | grep -q "upgrade"; then
+                has_upgrade=true
+            fi
+        fi
+
+        if ! $has_upgrade; then
+            log_debug "Skipping $tool_name: no upgrade command"
+            ((skipped++))
+            results+=("{\"tool\": \"$tool_name\", \"status\": \"skipped\", \"reason\": \"no upgrade command\"}")
+            continue
+        fi
+
+        local args=""
+        $build_from_source && args+=" --build-from-source"
+        $dry_run && args+=" --dry-run"
+
+        # shellcheck disable=SC2086
+        if upgrade_verify_tool "$tool_name" $args; then
+            ((passed++))
+            results+=("{\"tool\": \"$tool_name\", \"status\": \"passed\"}")
+        else
+            ((failed++))
+            results+=("{\"tool\": \"$tool_name\", \"status\": \"failed\"}")
+        fi
+    done
+
+    # Summary
+    log_info "Upgrade verification complete: $passed passed, $failed failed, $skipped skipped"
+
+    [[ $failed -eq 0 ]]
+}
+
+# Get upgrade verification results as JSON
+upgrade_verify_json() {
+    local tool_name="${1:-}"
+    local build_from_source=false
+
+    shift || true
+    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+        case "$1" in
+            --build-from-source) build_from_source=true; shift ;;
+            *) shift ;;
+        esac
+    done
+
+    local result status=0
+    local output exit_code platform
+
+    platform="$(uname -s | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')/$(uname -m)"
+
+    if [[ -n "$tool_name" ]]; then
+        # Single tool
+        local bin_path
+        bin_path=$(command -v "$tool_name" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
+
+        if [[ -z "$bin_path" ]]; then
+            cat << EOF
+{
+  "tool": "$tool_name",
+  "status": "error",
+  "error": "Tool not found in PATH",
+  "platform": "$platform"
+}
+EOF
+            return 1
+        fi
+
+        output=$(timeout "$UPGRADE_VERIFY_TIMEOUT" "$bin_path" upgrade --check 2>&1) || exit_code=$?
+
+        local found_asset=false
+        if echo "$output" | grep -qi "up.to.date\|no update\|already.*latest\|current version\|found.*asset\|download.*available\|update.*available"; then
+            found_asset=true
+            status=0
+        elif [[ ${exit_code:-0} -eq 0 ]]; then
+            found_asset=true
+            status=0
+        else
+            found_asset=false
+            status=1
+        fi
+
+        cat << EOF
+{
+  "tool": "$tool_name",
+  "status": "$([ $status -eq 0 ] && echo "passed" || echo "failed")",
+  "platform": "$platform",
+  "found_asset": $found_asset,
+  "exit_code": ${exit_code:-0},
+  "output": $(echo "$output" | jq -Rs '.')
+}
+EOF
+        return $status
+    else
+        # All tools
+        echo '{"tools": ['
+        local first=true
+        local repos_dir="${ACT_REPOS_DIR:-${DSR_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.config/dsr}/repos.d}"
+
+        for config_file in "$repos_dir"/*.yaml; do
+            [[ -f "$config_file" ]] || continue
+            [[ "$(basename "$config_file")" == _* ]] && continue
+
+            tool_name=$(basename "$config_file" .yaml)
+            $first || echo ","
+            first=false
+
+            upgrade_verify_json "$tool_name" 2>/dev/null || true
+        done
+
+        echo '], "platform": "'"$platform"'"}'
+    fi
+}
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Internal Helpers
+# ============================================================================
+
+# Find repository directory for a tool
+_upgrade_find_repo_dir() {
+    local tool_name="$1"
+    local repos_dir="${ACT_REPOS_DIR:-${DSR_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.config/dsr}/repos.d}"
+    local config_file="$repos_dir/$tool_name.yaml"
+
+    if [[ ! -f "$config_file" ]]; then
+        # Try standard location
+        local default_path="/data/projects/$tool_name"
+        if [[ -d "$default_path" ]]; then
+            echo "$default_path"
+            return 0
+        fi
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    # Get local_path from config
+    local local_path
+    if command -v yq &>/dev/null; then
+        local_path=$(yq -r '.local_path // ""' "$config_file" 2>/dev/null)
+    else
+        local_path=$(grep '^local_path:' "$config_file" 2>/dev/null | \
+                    sed 's/local_path:\s*//' | tr -d '"' | tr -d "'")
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -n "$local_path" && -d "$local_path" ]]; then
+        echo "$local_path"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    return 1
+}
+
+# Build tool from source
+_upgrade_build_tool() {
+    local tool_name="$1"
+    local repo_dir="$2"
+    local tmpdir
+    tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
+
+    local bin_path="$tmpdir/$tool_name"
+
+    if [[ -f "$repo_dir/go.mod" ]]; then
+        # Go project
+        log_debug "Building Go project..."
+        if [[ -d "$repo_dir/cmd/$tool_name" ]]; then
+            go build -o "$bin_path" "$repo_dir/cmd/$tool_name" 2>/dev/null
+        elif [[ -f "$repo_dir/main.go" ]]; then
+            go build -o "$bin_path" "$repo_dir" 2>/dev/null
+        fi
+    elif [[ -f "$repo_dir/Cargo.toml" ]]; then
+        # Rust project
+        log_debug "Building Rust project..."
+        cargo build --release --manifest-path "$repo_dir/Cargo.toml" 2>/dev/null
+        local target_path="$repo_dir/target/release/$tool_name"
+        if [[ -f "$target_path" ]]; then
+            cp "$target_path" "$bin_path"
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -x "$bin_path" ]]; then
+        echo "$bin_path"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    rm -rf "$tmpdir"
+    return 1
+}
+
+# Export functions
+export -f upgrade_verify_tool upgrade_verify_all upgrade_verify_json
+export -f _upgrade_find_repo_dir _upgrade_build_tool
